name: arXiv
description: A free, open-access preprint repository where physicists publish papers before (and often instead of) formal journal publication. The primary way physics knowledge is shared.
whyUseIt: arXiv is where new physics results appear first — often months before journal publication. Reading arXiv daily is how active physicists stay current. It covers all areas of physics, mathematics, computer science, and related fields. Over 2 million papers and counting.
fieldPosition: Universal — the primary literature source for all of physics
physicistWorkflow: Check new listings daily in your sub-field, read abstracts, download relevant papers, and track citations.
supervisorExpects: Regular reading of arXiv papers in your research area and awareness of current developments.
computationalImpact: Provides instant, free access to the latest research — eliminates journal paywalls as a barrier to knowledge.

vocabulary.definition: A paper shared before formal peer review and journal publication
A unique identifier for each paper, e.g. 2301.12345

beginnerMistakes: Not checking arXiv regularly and missing important new results
Treating preprints as peer-reviewed (they are not — read critically)
